Every time this change is abandoned and re pushed, it becomes a new code review. Comments on a review should be preserved and addressed. Once they are addressed, a new patch is pushed to the same code review.

That is the nature of reviewing code. Sometimes there will be many changes required. This is normal. The history of each patch set preserves the changes made to the original. If you abandon and re submit, these are lost.

> https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/dcaegen2/services/ +/130040

No You can continue with the patch.
The process to follow is:
Checkout your local branch,
make changes,
commit the new patch for review,
request a review,
if changes are needed - 
address the changes on your local branch,
address the comments, if any on gerrit,
git add .
git commit --amend
git push origin HEAD:refs/for/master

This will create a new patch set on your active code changes on gerrit
